compliance_degree.reason: The violated requirement checks for the existence of the activity label "activate screeen lock". Renaming the existing near-matching activity "activate screen lock" directly satisfies the required existence condition.

resolution_context_consistency.reason: The change is unrelated to the authentication XOR branch and does not introduce a path where "Unlock screen" follows the condition "not data.authenticated".

change_cost.reason: Only one localized rename operation is required.

structural_quality_impact.reason: The process structure, ordering, branching, and parallel behavior remain unchanged. Only the activity label is modified.

change_location.reason: The changed activity is located in the initial parallel screen-locking part of the process before access is requested.

compliance_degree.reason: The required activity label "activate screeen lock" is inserted into the process, satisfying the existence requirement.

resolution_context_consistency.reason: The inserted activity is in the screen-locking part of the process and does not affect the authentication decision or the placement of "Unlock screen".

change_cost.reason: A single insertion is required, but it adds a new activity rather than only correcting an existing label.

structural_quality_impact.reason: The existing process behavior is mostly preserved, but the new activity may introduce redundancy because it is nearly identical to the existing "activate screen lock" activity.

change_location.reason: The new activity is added in the initial parallel screen-locking section of the process.

compliance_degree.reason: The required activity label "activate screeen lock" is introduced before the existing "activate screen lock" activity, satisfying the existence requirement.

resolution_context_consistency.reason: The change remains outside the authentication XOR structure and therefore should not violate the context requirement concerning "Unlock screen" and "not data.authenticated".

change_cost.reason: Only one insertion operation is required.

structural_quality_impact.reason: The insertion preserves the original activity but may create a redundant or confusing pair of nearly identical screen-locking activities.

change_location.reason: The modification occurs in the initial screen-locking part of the process before access is requested.

best_resolution_reason: RS1 is the most localized and least costly process change. It fully satisfies the detected existence requirement while preserving the process structure and avoiding any impact on the authentication-related resolution context requirement. The only caveat is that the required label appears to contain a spelling error; if this is unintended, correcting the requirement itself would be preferable outside the supported process-change operations.

alternative_suggestion.reason: The detected violation appears to be caused by a spelling mismatch: the process already contains "activate screen lock", while the requirement checks for "activate screeen lock". Correcting the compliance requirement is not one of the supported process change operations.
alternative_suggestion.conceptual_solution: Update requirement R2 from exists(tree, 'activate screeen lock') to exists(tree, 'activate screen lock') so that it matches the existing process activity label.
alternative_suggestion.expected_compliance_impact: This would resolve the violation without changing the process model and would avoid introducing a misspelled or duplicate activity.
